channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Ireland allows foreigners travel with expired residence cards until Aug 31
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ireland permits foreign residents to travel with expired Irish Residence Permit cards until August 31, 2026, due to renewal backlogs. Learn more about this

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: McLaren have confirmed they will bring a "significant upgrade package" across the upcoming double-header of races in Belgium and Hungary before Formula 1's break.

item.title: Will The Open tee times change if England reach the World Cup final?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: R&A chief executive Mark Darbon has confirmed that plans are "under review" on whether to change Sunday's final-round tee times at The Open, should England reach the FIFA World Cup final.

item.description: Abia State records the lowest maternal and infant mortality rates in the first quarter of 2026, making it the best-performing read more Abia records lowest maternal, infant mortality rates in Q1 2026

item.title: Israeli Fire Kills Nine In Gaza, Including 10-Year-Old- Officials
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: An Israeli strike and gunfire killed at least nine Palestinians, including ‌a 10-year-old boy, and a senior Hamas police officer, in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, Gazan health and police officials said. The deaths add to a toll of more than 1,100 Palestinians killed by Israeli attacks since an October

item.title: Muslim-Muslim ticket: Stop Islamic agenda, we’re ready for self-defence – Dachomo to Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Regional Chairman of the Church of Christ in Nations, COCIN, in Barkin Ladi Local Government Area of Plateau State, Rev. Ezekiel Dachomo, has accused President Bola Tinubu of sponsoring an Islamic agenda in Nigeria. Dachomo alleged that Tinubu is trying to use the Muslim-Muslim ticket to “

item.title: 23.2% of Plateau children out of school, govt mulls sanctions for parents
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The government said it was considering stricter measures, including sanctions for parents who fail to enrol their children in school, as part of efforts to reverse the trend. The post 23.2% of Plateau children out of school, govt mulls sanctions for parents appeared first on Vanguard News.
